title: For the Parent Who Got It Wrong
scripture_text: Honor your father and your mother, that your days may be long in the land.
reflection: They were not perfect. They were not even always good. But they were yours, and you came from them, and the long work of love is what the Lord asks.
body:
You did not get what you needed. They were too busy, or too wounded, or too young, or too hard. You learned to perform. You learned to be the easy child. You learned to not need.

That was not okay. And they were, probably, doing the best they could with what they had been given.

You do not have to be at peace with it yet. You do not have to make them into a saint. You only have to do the long, slow work of letting them be human, and letting yourself be free of the role you took on to survive them.

Honor them. Not because they earned it. Because the work of honoring is what frees you.
prayer: Lord, help me to honor them, and to not let what was done to me be done to anyone else. Amen.
